content.problemStatement:
Write a C program that calculates and prints the power of a number.

You are required to implement a function named `power` that takes two integers as arguments:

base:base:base:  the number to be raised

exponent:exponent:exponent: the power to which the base is raised

The function should return the result of raising basebasebase to the power of exponentexponentexponent.


Input Format

A single line containing two integers: basebasebase and exponentexponentexponent


Output Format

Print a single integer representing basebasebase raised to the power of exponentexponentexponent
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers: base (1 ≤ base ≤ 100) and exponent (‑100 ≤ exponent ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the integer value of base raised to exponent. The result is truncated toward zero (fractional part discarded).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Any number to the power 0 is 1.

solution.approach: Use binary (fast) exponentiation for non‑negative exponents, which runs in O(log exponent) time. For a negative exponent the exact value is 1/(base^{|exponent|}); after truncating toward zero the result is 0 for any base>1 and 1 when base==1. This avoids floating‑point arithmetic and overflow. The overall complexity per test case is O(log |exponent|) with O(1) extra space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Any number to the power 0 is 1.
